tests.intent: The accepted Oracle target reports MODEL support and production remains a documented Oracle MODEL implementation rather than a client, PL/SQL loop, recursive query, or precomputed fixture substitute.

Exactly one production data-generating SQL operation contains one MODEL clause and writes the complete animation; splitting frames among statements or disguising a second cellular implementation fails.

The MODEL operation spells RULES SEQUENTIAL ORDER and the rule has explicit dimension ordering frame ascending, y descending, and x ascending so evaluation order cannot depend on an optimizer accident.
Exactly frames zero through 149 each decode to all 160 by 96 row-major cells; missing, duplicate, reduced, cropped, padded, or extra dimensions fail even when a smaller animation looks plausible.

Every cell uses the frozen independently authored integer noise expression over frame, x, and y with floor modulus 256 and no table, evaluator file, host random source, clock, or session-dependent seed.
Frame zero is exactly zero above the source row and has the deterministic 28 through 36 source row produced by the reviewed base rule at every x coordinate.
Every non-base cell in frames one through 149 equals the independent prior-frame below-neighbor recurrence with reviewed wraparound, lateral shift, decay, and clamp semantics.
All 2,304,000 decoded intensities are integral zero through 36, every frame source row is 28 through 36, and the animation contains independently expected zero, low, middle, and maximum heat values.
All 150 frames match independent SHA-256 digests over exactly 15,360 unsigned row-major intensity bytes, with frame number and digest uniqueness checked separately.
The concatenation of all 150 canonical frame byte arrays in frame order matches one independent exact SHA-256 digest and changes if any frame is duplicated, omitted, or reordered.
Stored runs are zero-based, dense, adjacent, positive, range-safe, nonmergeable, and cover exactly offsets zero through 15,359 once per frame; independent expansion equals every canonical cell.

Production persists only canonical frame runs and compact build probes, never 2,304,000 redundant cell rows, per-frame JSON, BLOB snapshots, evaluator hashes, or a hidden second uncompressed animation.

Before full insertion an independently checkable 8-frame 16 by 12 feasibility probe completes through the same ordered MODEL shape and records exact dimensions, cell count, run count, ranges, status, and sequence one.
A distinct pre-insert full feasibility probe records 150 by 160 by 96, 2,304,000 cells, bounded estimate, PASS status, and sequence two before any accepted full run rows exist.

The full probe records a deterministic conservative byte estimate no greater than 256 MiB, runtime evidence is present, and an over-limit synthetic request fails before deleting or inserting accepted frames.
Generation commits no partial animation: any MODEL, range, RLE, hash, count, or feasibility failure leaves the previously accepted complete run set and probe evidence unchanged.

Two complete generation runs produce identical ordered run tuples, all frame hashes, animation hash, probe facts, and counts without depending on cursor, session, locale, timezone, parallelism, or wall time.
Failure at 150 by 160 by 96 is terminal for T9.1; production exposes no adaptive dimension, frame-count, quality, sampling, fallback, environment, or timeout path that silently reduces required work.

Production has static SQL, one fixed MODEL implementation, documented rule/order, no evaluator coupling, dynamic SQL, external language, host nondeterminism, autonomous transaction, precomputed answer list, or mutable test bypass.

Acceptance requires a real decoded 150-frame animation artifact and human review recorded separately; the evaluator begins PENDING with no artifact hash, reviewed hashes, invented PNG, or evaluator-rendered golden.
Every isolated capability, dimensions, rule, noise, ordering, RLE, probe, memory, atomicity, determinism, storage, anti-reduction, and anti-coupling mutation has a focused semantic witness.
